title: elymus repens subsp. atlantis
intro: Elymus repens subsp. atlantis, also known as the couch grass or quackgrass, is a perennial grass that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Elymus repens subsp. atlantis has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in holistic medicine and natural remedies.
